target.title: feat(whatsapp): expand live QA coverage
target.body: ## Summary¥n¥nExpands the WhatsApp live QA lane from a small smoke set into a broad regression lane that covers the extension's main channel capabilities after updates.¥n¥nThis PR also updates the WhatsApp QA driver and gateway-facing send surface so the live lane can exercise structured WhatsApp-specific sends instead of relying on private test-only hooks.¥n¥n## What Changed¥n¥n- Adds WhatsApp QA driver/send support for structured sends:¥n  - contact cards¥n  - locations¥n  - stickers¥n- Adds QA gateway/live-lane helpers for:¥n  - `send`¥n  - `poll`¥n  - `message.action`¥n  - workspace media fixtures¥n  - observed-message recording and post-send assertions¥n- Adds deterministic mock-provider support for WhatsApp long-final responses so chunk/final-message behavior can be asserted reliably.¥n- Updates WhatsApp auto-reply test harness mocks to implement the expanded listener send surface.¥n¥n## New WhatsApp QA Coverage¥n¥nThe WhatsApp QA catalog now covers 35 scenarios total.¥n¥nNew scenario families added in this branch include:¥n¥n- Basic DM/group smoke and gating:¥n  - canary¥n  - pairing gate¥n  - mention gating¥n  - top-level reply shape¥n  - restart/resume¥n- Native command UX:¥n  - `/help`¥n  - `/status`¥n  - `/commands`¥n  - `/tools compact`¥n  - `/whoami`¥n  - `/context list`¥n  - `/new`¥n- Usage/final-output behavior:¥n  - tool-only usage footer¥n  - long streamed final message accounting¥n  - long quoted reply chunking¥n- Reply/quote behavior:¥n  - reply-to mode quotes the triggering message¥n  - fresh sends do not reuse prior quote context¥n- Inbound media/structured messages:¥n  - image caption¥n  - audio preflight transcript¥n  - document caption¥n  - poll¥n  - location¥n  - contact send probe¥n  - sticker send probe¥n  - group audio gating¥n- Outbound send/action behavior:¥n  - image¥n  - document¥n  - audio/voice¥n  - multi-media¥n  - document filename preservation¥n  - native poll¥n  - message.action react¥n  - message.action upload-file¥n- Access control:¥n  - DM open¥n  - DM disabled¥n  - group open¥n  - group disabled¥n  - group allowlist block¥n- Native approvals:¥n  - exec allow-once¥n  - exec deny¥n  - exec reaction approval¥n  - plugin allow-once¥n- Status reactions:¥n  - observable WhatsApp status reactions¥n¥n## Default Lane Behavior¥n¥n- `live-frontier` remains intentionally small at 8 default scenarios for fast live smoke coverage.¥n- `mock-openai` now runs 29 deterministic WhatsApp scenarios by default.¥n- Approval and some heavier/blocking scenarios remain explicit-only unless selected by scenario ID.¥n¥nThe mocked-provider scenarios still run through the real WhatsApp transport; only the model/provider response is mocked so the lane can assert exact markers, chunk counts, and structured behavior without frontier-model variance.¥n¥n## Verification¥n¥nPassed locally after rebasing onto `origin/main`:¥n¥n- `pnpm test extensions/whatsapp/src/qa-driver.runtime.test.ts extensions/whatsapp/src/inbound/send-api.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/live-transports/whatsapp/whatsapp-live.runtime.test.ts extensions/qa-lab/src/providers/mock-openai/server.test.ts`¥n- `pnpm tsgo:extensions`¥n- `pnpm tsgo:extensions:test`¥n- `git diff --check`¥n¥nPassed via Blacksmith Testbox:¥n¥n- `pnpm check:changed`¥n¥n
